item1_entity.address: 7930 Jones Branch Drive, Suite 1100, McLean, Virginia 22102
parent_chain: Hilton Domestic Operating Company Inc. (Delaware corporation, formed 2016-07-12)
Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (Delaware corporation, formed 2010-03-18, NYSE: HLT)

pending_actions: AAAA Property Partners v. HFH — breach of contract (franchise termination)
In re Extended Stay Hotel Antitrust (N.D. Cal.) — class action, IDeaS price-fixing
Dai v. SAS/IDeaS (N.D. Cal.) — class action, IDeaS price-fixing
Segal v. Amadeus (N.D. Ill.) — class action, data exchange
Portillo v. CoStar/STR (W.D. Wash.) — class action, competitive data exchange

item6_ongoing_fees.total_core_pct: 10%+ of GRR before loyalty and tech

item7_investment.excludes: real property, market studies, insurance, interest

item12_territory.restricted_area_scope: Hampton Brand only, not other Hilton brands

item17_contract.venue: E.D. Virginia or Fairfax County, VA; fallback NY

item21_financials.tax_status: Disregarded entity for US income tax — no US income tax provision. All taxable income included in Hilton's computation.
item21_financials.acquisitions: Graduate brand franchise contracts acquired May 2024 for ~$85M, 15-year amortization (asset acquisition).
item21_financials.debt_guarantee: ALL entity assets and franchise contracts pledged as collateral for Hilton parent debt (maturing 2025-2033). Unconditional and irrevocable guarantee.
item21_financials.related_party: Centralized cash management by Hilton affiliates. Franchise deposits in lockbox with no entity access. Operator agreement with Hilton affiliate (no compensation paid 2022-2024). Contract acquisition costs ($376M gross) amortized as reduction of royalty fees.

unresolveds.description: Antitrust litigation (IDeaS, Amadeus, STR) — potential systemic impact on required technology and pricing
No EBITDA or cost data in Item 19 — franchisees cannot assess profitability from FDD alone
Restricted Area negotiability unknown — FDD provides no guidance on what is typically granted
Program Fee increase ceiling ambiguity — 'current rate + 1% of GRR' interpretation unclear
Service Quality Improvement Program updates pending later this year
Technology refresh costs have no cap on frequency or cost
Confidentiality clause restricts former franchisee communication
Hampton Inn brand contraction — first net decrease in recent years (-1 in 2024)

contradictions.description: Total hotel count discrepancy: Item 19 states 2,368; Item 20 sums to 2,369
contradictions.resolution_status: Minor — likely rounding or timing difference

contradictions.description: Item 20 Table 1 vs Table 3 start-of-2022 count difference (1,313 vs 1,323 for Hampton Inn)
contradictions.resolution_status: Unexplained within franchised-only scope — possibly data collection timing

contradictions.description: Financial statements Note 9 subsequent events evaluation date reads 'March 18, 2024' but should be March 18, 2025 (matching audit opinion date)

material_overrides.north_dakota: ALL liquidated damages deleted; ND law supersedes NY law; out-of-state venue deleted
material_overrides.minnesota: Liquidated damages prohibited; 90-day termination notice; no out-of-state litigation
material_overrides.california: Liquidated damages may be unenforceable; CA termination law may supersede
material_overrides.washington: Transfer fees limited to actual costs; non-compete restricted by income thresholds; no-poach void
material_overrides.michigan: Termination only for good cause; no out-of-state litigation; transfer denial only for good cause
